What is the main landmass of the Australian continent?
Mainland Australia
How long ago did Australian Aborigines occupy mainland Australia?
up to 50,000 to 60,000 years ago
What is the area of mainland Australia in square kilometers?
When did Australia fully separate from Antarctica?
around 53 million years ago.
When did Willem Janszoon first document his encounter with Australia?
What tectonic plate is Australia located on?
Indo-Australian tectonic plate
How long does the Southern Highlands experience snowfall during winter?
for more than half the year
What percentage of the Australian mainland do the Central Lowlands occupy?
approximately 25%
What are the three primary drainage basins included in the Central Lowlands?
Carpentaria Lowlands, Lake Eyre, and Murray-Darling Basin.
What is the elevation of Lake Eyre in meters below sea level?
50 meters below sea level
When was the Western Plateau formed?
approximately 144 to 65 million years ago during the Cretaceous period
What type of rock are the plateaus on the mainland made of?
igneous and metamorphic rock
What is the average elevation of the Plateau above sea level?
500 meters
What notable geographical features are included in the Plateau?
Uluru, Kata Tjuta, the Finke River, the Victorian Desert, Great Sandy Desert, and Gibson Desert.
What color does the Western Plateau appear?
red
When does the Northern Plateau experience heavy rainfall?
from November to April
Where is the Northern Plateau located compared to the Western Plateau?
in a more tropical region
What resources have accumulated in Australia due to its geological processes?
Mineral, oil, and gas resources.
What are some of the minerals and metals that Australia is one of the world's largest producers of?
iron ore, coal, bauxite, manganese, nickel, copper, gold, silver, cobalt, lead, uranium, opal, and diamond
What sources contributed to the majority of Australia's electricity generation as of 2021?
Renewable energy sources, particularly wind and solar.
As of 2024, has the Liberal National opposition ever controlled all of Mainland Australia at federal and state levels simultaneously?
No, the Liberal National opposition has never controlled all of Mainland Australia at federal and state levels simultaneously as of 2024.
When was Canberra officially named the capital of Australia?
12 March 1913
